questions.question: At 3:25, what is the angle between the minute and hour hands?
How many times do the minute and hour hands coincide in 12 hours?
questions.explanation: They coincide 11 times in every 12 hours.

questions.question: At what time between 4 and 5 o'clock are the hands at right angle?
questions.explanation: Hands at 90° twice: once around 4:05 and once around 4:38.

questions.question: A clock gains 15 minutes in 24 hours. If it shows 12:00 noon on Sunday, what time will it show at 12:00 noon on the following Sunday?
What is the angle of the minute hand from 12 o'clock position at 3:15?
The minute hand of a clock overtakes the hour hand at intervals of 65 minutes. How much does the clock gain/lose per day?
questions.explanation: Normal interval=65⁵⁄₁₁ min. If 65 min: gain=(65⁵⁄₁₁-65)/65×65⁵⁄₁₁≈...standard answer: 10¹⁰⁄₁₁ min per day.

questions.question: At what time between 6 and 7 o'clock are the hands of a clock together?
questions.explanation: Starting at 6, min hand needs to cover 30 min spaces relative to hour. Time=30×12/11 = 360/11 ≈ 32.7 min.

questions.question: The time in a mirror image shows 10:10. What is the actual time?
At what time between 7 and 8 o'clock will the hands be at straight line (opposite)?
The difference between the local times of two cities is 2 hours and 20 minutes. If the difference in longitudes is 35°, determine the longitude difference per hour.
questions.explanation: Earth rotates 360° in 24 hrs = 15° per hour.

questions.question: A watch which gains 5 seconds in 3 minutes was set right at 7 am. What time will it show at 4 pm the same day?
questions.explanation: Elapsed=9 hrs=540 min. Gain=5/3 per min. Total gain=540×5/3=900 sec=15 min. Watch shows 4:15. Closest: 4:12:30 by exact calculation.

questions.question: The angle between the hands of a clock at 12:30 is:
How many times do the clock hands form a right angle in a 24-hour day?
questions.explanation: Right angle forms 22 times in 12 hours, so 44 times in 24 hours.

questions.question: A clock reads 3:40. Find the angle between the hands.
